body: PT Bangun Kosambi Sukses Tbk (CBDK) announced a marketing sales acquisition of IDR 2.1 trillion for fiscal year 2024, driven by its commercial real estate business which contributed 71.4% to total marketing sales, up 78% from IDR 1.5 trillion. The company's residential and commercial product segments also saw significant sales, recording IDR 318 billion and IDR 308 billion, respectively, in 2024.
title: PT Bangun Kosambi Sukses Tbk announces IDR 2.1 trillion acquisition in marketing sales for fiscal year 2024.

body: Japfa Ltd, parent entity of PT Japfa Comfeed Indonesia Tbk (JPFA), will be taken private by the Santosa family through a scheme, where all minority shares will be acquired, with the family already controlling 55.43% of JPFA shares. The privatisation will not change the control portion of JPFA's shares and is expected to have no impact on the company's operational activities.
title: Japfa Ltd to be taken private by Santosa family through takeover of remaining shares.

body: The Indonesian government will issue its first retail government securities, ORI027, with a coupon rate of 6.65% to 6.75% and two tenors of 3 years and 6 years, offering a minimum investment of IDR 1 million and maximum investments of IDR 5 billion and IDR 10 billion, respectively. The offering period will take place from January 27 to February 20, 2025, and the bonds will be traded on the secondary market among domestic investors.
title: Indonesia to Issue First Retail Government Securities with 6.65-6.75% Coupon Rate This Year

body: Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati aims to maintain the 2025 state budget posture despite President Prabowo Subianto's order to cut government spending by IDR 306.69 trillion, with austerity measures only affecting government spending and not changing state revenue projections. The government plans to implement spending efficiency measures to save budgets in inefficient ministries and state institutions, as outlined in Presidential Instruction Number 1/2025.
title: Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati to Maintain 2025 State Budget Posture Despite President's Order to Cut Government Spending.

body: PT Jakarta Kyoei Steel Works Tbk (JKSW) plans to conduct a share buyback at IDR 59 per share for six months starting January 30, 2025, to fulfill the cancellation of the company's listing. The buyback aims to reduce the number of shareholders to less than 50 parties, with the repurchase method carried out through the Securities Administration Bureau.
title: Jakarta Kyoei Steel Works to Conduct Share Buyback to Fulfil Delisting Requirements.

body: PT Sumber Mineral Global Abadi TBK (SMGA)'s share price has fallen 42.85% to IDR59-60 per share since its initial public offering (IPO) at IDR105, with net proceeds of IDR182.05 billion allocated for nickel and coal procurement. As of last week, the company has realized IDR164.05 billion of the IPO proceeds, leaving IDR18.03 billion in remaining funds.
title: PT Sumber Mineral Global Abadi's share price falls 42.85% since IPO, currently trading between IDR 59-60 per share.

body: Shikoku Electric Power Company (TYO:9507) has acquired 25% of PT Hero Global Investment Tbk (HGII) shares for IDR 325 billion, changing HGII's shareholder composition to 55% founders, 25% Shikoku, and 20% public shares. The partnership aims to accelerate HGII's renewable energy development in Indonesia, targeting 100 MW capacity by 2031, with Shikoku's expertise expected to enhance HGII's profitability and support Indonesia's decarbonisation efforts.
title: Shikoku Electric Power Company Acquires 25% Stake in PT Hero Global Investment TBK to Boost Renewable Energy Development in Indonesia.

body: Multiple investors, including Morgan Stanley Co International Plc, PT Samuel Sekuritas Indonesia, and Bank Singapore Limited, increased their stakes in various Indonesian companies, such as PT MD Pictures Tbk, PT Artha Mahiya Investama Tbk, and PT Digital Mediatama Maxima Tbk, with ownership percentages ranging from 5.26% to 77.18%. Meanwhile, other investors, including HSBC-Fund Svs A/C Chengdong Investment Corp-Self and Andreas Utomo, sold shares in companies like PT Bumi Resources Tbk, PT Samator Indo Gas Tbk, and PT Aman Agrindo Tbk, resulting in decreased ownership percentages.
title: Multiple investors adjust their stakes in various Indonesian companies, including film production, financial services, and mining firms.

body: PT Autopedia Sukses Lestari Tbk (ASLC) has allocated IDR 20-30 billion for capital expenditure in 2024, which will be used to expand Caroline.id, its used car segment, by opening new showrooms, with revenue surging 35% year-on-year to IDR 416.76 billion as of September 2024. The company expects Caroline.id's total sales to surpass 2023's figure of 3,135 units, driven by the strategic focus on the used car segment amid challenging economic conditions.
title: PT Autopedia Sukses Lestari to Allocate IDR 20-30 Billion for Caroline.id Expansion

body: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) distributed loans worth IDR 922 trillion in 2024, a 13.8% increase from 2023, with a non-performing loan ratio of 1.8%, driven by corporate, commercial, SME, and consumer loans. The bank's operating income reached IDR 107.4 trillion, with a net profit of IDR 54.8 trillion, despite provision expenses of IDR 2 trillion, thanks to customer trust and government support.
title: Bank Central Asia Distributes IDR 922 Trillion in Loans in 2024, Sees 13.8% Growth and Low NPL Ratio.

body: PT Bumi Serpong Damai Tbk (BSDE) has settled its senior notes worth USD 88.91 million, which were issued in 2020, and there is no remaining outstanding amount after the settlement. The company had previously settled a portion of the notes worth USD 211.08 million in November 2023, and its outstanding bond was recorded at IDR 1.95 trillion as of September 2023.
title: PT Bumi Serpong Damai Settles USD 88.91 Million Senior Notes Ahead of Maturity Date
